curricula.name: IB
curricula.examStructure: PYP (Primary Years Programme), MYP (Middle Years Programme), DP (Diploma Programme)
curricula.parentProfile: Values inquiry-based learning and global citizenship. Often considering university abroad.

curricula.name: IGCSE
curricula.examStructure: Two-year program (ages 14-16), final exams set by Cambridge or Edexcel. Core and extended levels available.
curricula.parentProfile: Wants a recognized British qualification. Often planning A Levels or university in the UK.

curricula.name: A Level
curricula.examStructure: Two-year program (ages 16-18), 3-4 subjects. AS Level at end of first year, A Level at end of second year.
curricula.parentProfile: Focused on university admissions. Needs strong grades for competitive courses.

curricula.name: Cambridge Curriculum
curricula.examStructure: Cambridge Primary, Lower Secondary, IGCSE, and A Level progression. Checkpoint exams at end of primary and lower secondary.
curricula.parentProfile: Wants a structured, internationally recognized curriculum with clear progression.

curricula.name: British Curriculum
curricula.examStructure: Key Stages 1-4, leading to GCSE/IGCSE and A Level. National Curriculum framework with some school variation.
curricula.parentProfile: British or Commonwealth families. Familiar with UK system. May be returning to UK for university.

curricula.name: American Curriculum
curricula.examStructure: Common Core or school-specific standards. Grades K-12. AP courses in high school. SAT/ACT for university.
curricula.parentProfile: American families or families targeting US universities. Values broad education and extracurriculars.

curricula.name: Australian Curriculum
curricula.examStructure: Foundation to Year 10 Australian Curriculum. Some schools offer HSC or VCE in senior years.
curricula.parentProfile: Australian families or families planning to return to Australia. Familiar with Australian system.

curricula.name: International School Support
curricula.examStructure: Varies by school. Support for school-specific curriculum, assessments, and projects.
curricula.parentProfile: Parents whose children attend international schools and need extra support outside school hours.

curricula.name: Homeschool Support
curricula.examStructure: Flexible. Families choose curriculum (IB, IGCSE, A Level, American, or mixed). Tutors support delivery and exam registration.
curricula.parentProfile: Families educating at home. May be digital nomads, relocating families, or families seeking alternative education.

curricula.name: English as an Additional Language
targetSchools: All international schools with EAL programs
curricula.examStructure: No formal exam. Progress measured by language proficiency (CEFR levels, school assessments, IELTS if needed).
curricula.parentProfile: Families where English is not the first language. Children need support to access international curriculum.

curricula.name: Thai Language Support
targetSchools: International schools with Thai language programs
curricula.examStructure: School-specific Thai language assessments. Some students prepare for Thai proficiency tests.
curricula.parentProfile: Expat families wanting children to learn Thai. Mixed families. Families planning long-term stay in Thailand.

curricula.name: Exam Preparation
curricula.examStructure: IGCSE, A Level, IB, SAT, ACT, entrance exams. Focus on exam technique, past papers, and time management.
curricula.parentProfile: Parents with children approaching exams. High anxiety. Needs structured revision and confidence building.

curricula.name: SEN and Learning Support
targetSchools: All international schools with SEN departments
curricula.examStructure: Varies by student. May include accommodations for exams. Focus on learning strategies, not just content.
curricula.parentProfile: Parents of children with learning differences. Needs patient, specialist support. Often frustrated with standard tutoring.
